mnemonics: CIA (Continuous Internal Assessment) uses AI to Check, Improve, and Adapt models constantly.
Think of Continuous Evaluation like a car's GPS: it's always recalculating the route (model) based on new traffic data (feedback).
Forgetting CIE is like only checking a cake's recipe once; you need to taste (evaluate) it continuously as you bake (train) to make it perfect.
keyPoints: Continuous Internal Evaluation is an ongoing process of assessing an AI model's performance during its operational phase, not just after initial training.
It relies on a feedback loop where real-world data and outcomes are used to monitor for model drift, bias, or degradation in accuracy.
The goal is to enable the model to adapt and improve over time, ensuring it remains effective and relevant in a changing environment.
commonMistakes: Confusing Continuous Evaluation with the initial training/testing phase; it happens after deployment.
Assuming a model, once deployed, will perform perfectly forever without needing ongoing monitoring and maintenance.
Failing to establish a robust and automated feedback mechanism to collect new data for the evaluation process.
